# Daily Market Brief — Wednesday, May 6, 2026

*Auto-generated | Portfolio snapshot: April 21, 2026 | EUR/USD: ~1.179*

---

## 1. What Changed Since Yesterday

**1. AMD Blowout Q1 Earnings — AI Semiconductor Cycle Intact**
AMD reported Q1 2026 revenue of $10.25B (+38% YoY), EPS $1.37 vs $1.25 expected, and Q2 2026 guidance of ~$11.2B (above consensus). The Data Center segment surged +57% YoY to $5.8B. Stock rallied ~15% after-hours on May 5. This is a direct and powerful catalyst for your Micron (MU), SanDisk (SNDK), Lumentum (LITE), Credo (CRDO), and VanEck Semiconductor ETF positions. The AI infrastructure buildout is not slowing.

**2. Iran-US Ceasefire Holds Precariously — Oil Falls 4% on Pentagon Reassurance**
Monday-Tuesday saw the highest-intensity hostilities since the April 8 ceasefire: Iran fired on US Navy ships, US shot down 7 Iranian military boats, and UAE suffered renewed drone/missile attacks. Despite this, Pentagon chief Hegseth confirmed the ceasefire is "not over." Trump paused the Strait of Hormuz escort mission to allow time for negotiations. Oil fell ~4% on May 5 (Brent: $109.87, WTI: $102.27) but remains elevated vs pre-war levels. The "dual blockade" — US blockading Iranian ports AND Iran blockading the Persian Gulf — creates persistent energy price risk.

**3. Markets on 5-Week Winning Streak — Record Highs, VIX Compressing**
S&P 500 at 7,259 (+0.81%), Nasdaq at 25,326 (+1.03%), Dow at 49,298 (+0.73%). The VIX fell to 17.45 (-4.59%) — fear is easing. However: Fed held rates at 3.5–3.75% at April meeting with an unusually divided 8-4 vote, Powell's term expires May 15, and Kevin Warsh (more hawkish) takes over. Watch for EUR/USD volatility and potential bank stock repricing around this transition.

## 2. Three Concrete Actions for Today

**Action 1 — TRIM Micron (MU): Sell 20 shares at ~$675 (≈€11,440 proceeds)**
RSI at 76 is overbought. The stock is +50.6% from the April 21 portfolio reference. AMD's blowout earnings are already priced in. Place a limit sell for 20 shares at $675+. This locks in partial gains (~€13,449 estimated P&L on the full position) while retaining 50 shares for continued AI upside. Redeploy proceeds as USD cash for upcoming watchlist opportunities.

**Action 2 — TRIM SanDisk (SNDK): Sell 8 shares at ~$1,400 (≈€9,500 proceeds)**
RSI at 82 is extreme (very overbought). You are AT the Cantor analyst price target of $1,400. YTD gain ~600% makes this the top S&P 500 performer. Asymmetric risk is now to the downside. Sell 8 of 27 shares. Retain 19 shares for ongoing AI storage demand upside. Note: consult tax advisor on capital gains implications of this extraordinary gain.

**Action 3 — Set Buy Alert on ATI at $145 (target 35 shares, ≈€4,300)**
ATI Inc just beat Q1 EPS ($1.00 vs $0.88), raised 2026 guidance, and has a record order backlog — driven by defense/aerospace specialty metals demand. 8 analysts have Strong Buy consensus with $158.75 target. At current ~$155, risk/reward is thin. A pullback to $145 offers 9-10% upside to target. Use proceeds from MU/SNDK trims. Set a conditional limit buy at $145.
